text.title: IaC CI/CD Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `IaC CI/CD Patterns`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every PR touching `*.tf`/`pulumi/*` MUST attach a machine-readable `plan` artefact; merging without plan is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a recorded plan, reviewers approve blindly and apply surprises slip in.

rule.statement: `apply` MUST run only on the protected branch (`main`); apply-from-PR is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Apply-from-PR allows arbitrary code on a forked branch to mutate prod.

rule.statement: CI MUST authenticate to cloud via WIF/OIDC; static long-lived cloud credentials in CI secrets are forbidden.

rationale.source: GCP WIF docs, AWS GitHub OIDC docs

rule.rationale: Static keys are the dominant credential-leak vector.

rule.statement: A scheduled (nightly) drift-detect job MUST run `plan` against production state and alert on non-empty changes.
rule.rationale: Without drift detection, console drift accumulates until a noisy `apply` reverts it.

rule.statement: Promotion order dev → staging → prod MUST be enforced (manual gate before prod); apply directly to prod is forbidden.
If `Applies If` preconditions fail OR any `Skip If` trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
